old_claim.note: Advanced Chemistry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the electrochemical and alumina/cryolite chemistry relationship is represented through the canonical aluminum_production_hall_heroult process node.

source_shape.source_locator: ACS Hall-Héroult landmark describes aluminum metal production by electrochemistry: alumina dissolved in molten cryolite and reduced by electric current.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The relevant chemistry and electrochemistry belong inside the Hall-Héroult process, not as a separate direct edge on industrial smelting.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports removing the broad advanced_chemistry edge because the active smelting node now depends directly on the process node that already carries the chemical method.

ontology_before: The graph duplicated the chemistry relationship on both the process-like aluminum_smelting node and the canonical Hall-Héroult process node.
ontology_after: The graph routes the chemistry relationship through aluminum_production_hall_heroult and keeps aluminum_smelting as the industrial operation layer.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct aluminum_smelting -> advanced_chemistry edge removed.
would_reject_if: The Hall-Héroult process node stops representing the electrochemical reduction method.
A more specific alumina-cryolite-electrolyte node is added and becomes the direct prerequisite.
short_reason: The broad chemistry edge duplicated the process node's electrochemical mechanism.
